entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study on food security and soil management was conducted

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A period of agricultural development that brought food sovereignty to India

entities.name: FOOD SOVEREIGNTY
entities.description: The ability of a state to define its own food and agriculture policies

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops for food and other uses

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The continued capacity of soil to function as a vital living ecosystem

entities.name: QUALITATIVE INTERVIEWS
entities.description: A research method used to gather detailed information and construct mental models

entities.name: MENTAL MODELS
entities.description: The internal representations or perspectives held by stakeholders regarding soil health and food security

entities.name: PERI-URBAN WATERSHED
entities.description: The specific study site located on the outskirts of an urban area

entities.name: INTEGRATED FARMER PARTICIPATORY WATERSHED MANAGEMENT MODEL
entities.description: A management model for watersheds involving farmer participation

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing soil nutrients to optimize crop growth

entities.name: SOIL SODICITY
entities.description: A soil condition characterized by high levels of exchangeable sodium

entities.name: PLANT HEALTH
entities.description: The state of plants being free from disease and nutrient deficiencies

entities.name: HUMAN HEALTH
entities.description: The overall physical and mental well-being of people

entities.name: FOOD QUANTITY
entities.description: The volume or amount of food available for consumption

entities.name: FOOD QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional value, safety, and overall standard of food

entities.name: CULTURAL ACCEPTABILITY
entities.description: A dimension of food security referring to whether food is acceptable within a specific culture

relationships.target: INTEGRATED FARMER PARTICIPATORY WATERSHED MANAGEMENT MODEL
